system_prompt_preview:
# Manager

You are a workflow coordinator that routes tasks to the correct agents, manages handoffs between steps, and ensures each agent receives the right inputs in the right order.

## When to use this agent

Use the manager for **ad-hoc orchestration** — when a goal needs to be broken into agent tasks at runtime and no predefined workflow YAML exists for it. If a workflow YAML already covers the task, execute it directly through the workflow engine; do not route through the manager.

The man
task_preview:
Goal: Read the /Users/ADMIN/Documents/UNI SYDNEY/5. SYD SPRING 2026/QBUS3600/Main project/qbus3600_abs-zombies/Shared    
 Modelling/Unified NOTEBOOK/UNIFICATION_INSTRUCTION.txt and do the task as instructed

Available agent roster:
  [general] academic_researcher: Builds a reference library of 15–25 targeted and standby sources: 60% matched to specific findings, 40% broader coverage across supplied research areas
  [general] business_insights_writer: Translates analytical findings into business language: produces an executive summary and a business insights section with claim-to-source linking and actionable recommendations
  [general] chunk_extractor: Reads a Jupyter notebook and a notebook map, then extracts findings into structured chunk files — one per chunk — covering preprocessing, descriptive stats, relationships, and severity-tagged business signals, for use by downstream writing agents
  [general] citation_verifier: Resolves unsupported citation flags and reviewer-flagged cit

content:
1	================================================================================
2	PROJECT CONTEXT — READ THIS FIRST
3	================================================================================
4	
5	COURSE      : QBUS3600 — Business Analytics and Data Science (University of Sydney)
6	TEAM        : ABS-Zombies (6 members: Kaden, Sol, Tiff, Rey, Tre, Julie)
7	TASK TYPE   : Binary classification competition
8	DATASET     : UNICEF Australia donation sessions — predict whether a website
9	              visitor makes a donation (TARGET_purchased: 0 = no, 1 = yes)
10	CLASS SPLIT : ~77% class 1 (purchased), ~23% class 0 (did not purchase)
11	TRAINING    : 69,459 rows, 24 raw columns
12	HOLDOUT     : 3,674 rows, same 24 raw columns, no target label
13	PRIMARY METRIC : Macro F1 (unweighted average of class-0 F1 and class-1 F1)
14	DIRECTORY Reference: qbus3600_abs-zombies/Shared Modelling/United NOTEBOOK
15	
16	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	WHERE WE ARE IN THE PROJECT
18	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	
20	Stage 1 — Individual EDA (COMPLETE)
21	  Each team member independently explored the raw dataset, performed cleaning,
22	  and identified features. This produced several individual EDA notebooks and modelling files that have been migrated to Shared Modelling/United NOTEBOOK.
23	
24	Stage 2 — Shared EDA & Feature Engineering (COMPLETE)
25	  The team converged on one canonical cleaning pipeline (Clean + Interact.ipynb)
26	  which runs from the raw CSV and produces a single agreed cleaned file:
27	  UNICEF2026S1_clf_train_cleaned.csv (69,459 rows, 62 columns).
28	  This includes: source/medium/landing-page parsing, temporal feature extraction,
29	  Once_Ask imputation and binning, 30 statistically-tested interaction (ix__) columns.
30	  A second notebook (Clean cardinality.ipynb) was also created as a cardinality
31	  reduction experiment — part of shared propressin

1	================================================================================
2	QBUS3600 — BINARY CLASSIFICATION PIPELINE STRATEGY V2
3	Objective: Maximise Macro F1 Across All Models
4	Generated: 2026-05-05
5	================================================================================
6	
7	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8	1. FIXED PARAMETERS (non-negotiable constraints)
9	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10	
11	  Random seed       : 0  (applied to all splits, CV folds, model fitting,
12	                          resampling — every stochastic step)
13	  Train/Val split   : 70 / 30  (stratified on TARGET_purchased)
14	  Internal test set : NONE  (no separate internal test split; validation set
15	                          is the primary evaluation holdout)
16	  Primary metric    : Macro F1  (unweighted average of class-0 F1 and class-1 F1)
17	  Models in scope   : Logistic Regression (LR), Generalised Linear Model (GLM),
18	                      Generalised Additive Model (GAM), XGBoost, CatBoost,
19	                      LightGBM, Random Forest (RF), Neural Network (NN)
20	  Dataset           : UNICEF2026S1_clf_train_cleaned.csv (69,459 rows, 28 cols)
21	  Class imbalance   : Class 1 (purchaser) ≈ 77 %  |  Class 0 (non-purchaser) ≈ 23 %
22	  Feature scope     : All models trained on the FULL set of cleaned + enhanced
23	                      features (no collapsing or reduction).  Each model receives
24	                      the complete engineered design matrix with all features intact:
25	                      OHE'd categoricals, standardised numerics, interaction terms.
26	
27	  Role of the 30 % val set:
28	    The validation set is a DEDICATED HOLDOUT used for two purposes only:
29	      (1) Threshold tuning — sweep threshold on val predictions to maximise
30	          macro F1; the selected threshold is locked.
31	      (2) Final model comparison — val macro F1

1	================================================================================
2	QBUS3600 — BINARY CLASSIFICATION PIPELINE STRATEGY
3	Objective: Maximise Macro F1 Across All Models
4	Generated: 2026-05-01
5	================================================================================
6	
7	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
8	0. AGENT SYSTEM CAPABILITY SNAPSHOT (what the pipeline can do)
9	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
10	
11	The agent system is a file-based multi-agent orchestration framework running on
12	the Anthropic Claude API. Each agent is a folder containing config.json +
13	system_prompt.md. Pipelines are YAML workflows. Two execution modes:
14	  - Workflow mode  →  run a named YAML pipeline directly
15	  - Task mode      →  free-form goal; manager routes through planning → execution
16	
17	What the existing qbus3600 agent roster can already do:
18	  M0  modeling_planner         : read data → produce modeling_plan.md
19	  M1  data_loader              : stratified split, schema validation, parquets
20	  M2  feature_engineer         : OHE, target-encoding, log1p, standardisation,
21	                                 interaction terms — fit on train only
22	  M3  model_specifier          : generate model_grid.json (family × reg × features)
23	  M4  statsmodels_fitter       : binomial GLM via statsmodels (inference focus)
24	  M5  sklearn_cv_fitter        : LR variants via sklearn + RepeatedStratifiedKFold
25	  M6  diagnostics              : VIF, residuals, ROC, PR, calibration, HL, bootstrap
26	  M7  model_comparator         : merge all results, rank, recommend winner
27	  M8  artifact_manager         : SHA-256 manifest, environment.json, README
28	  M9  notebook_assembler       : build reproducible end-to-end .ipynb
29	  M10 macro_f1_optimizer       : sweep class_weight × C × threshold for LR,
30	                                 produce macro_f1_grid.csv + plots

text:
I now have complete context. Let me produce the workflow plan.

---

# Workflow Plan — Unified QBUS3600 Notebook Assembly

## Goal
Build a single master Jupyter notebook (`Unified_Notebook.ipynb`) from 6 individual source notebooks/scripts per the UNIFICATION_INSTRUCTIONS.txt blueprint. The notebook covers 6 phases: data pipeline, shared infrastructure, 8 model families (3A–3H), model comparison leaderboard, holdout prediction + submission, and a reproducibility checklist. All outputs go to a structured `OUTPUTS/` tree.

## Working Directory
`/Users/ADMIN/Documents/UNI SYDNEY/5. SYD SPRING 2026/QBUS3600/Main project/qbus3600_abs-zombies/Shared Modelling/Unified NOTEBOOK/`

---

## Step Sequence

| Step | Agent | Task Instruction | Input Keys | Output Key | Depends On |
|------|-------|-----------------|------------|------------|------------|
| **1a** | `notebook_mapper` | Map `Clean + Interact.ipynb` — produce section index identifying cell ranges for: imports, path setup, raw load, row cleaning (cells 9–16), column cleaning (cells 19–65), interaction engineering (cells 91–105), save (cell 106), and EDA visualisation cells. Tag each with the Phase 1 sub-section (1.1–1.7) it maps to. | `source_notebook: Current notebooks/Clean + Interact.ipynb` | `eda_map` | — |
| **1b** | `notebook_mapper` | Map `Logistics.ipynb` — identify cell ranges for: data load, internal preprocessor, C-sweep loop, evaluate() function, baseline LR block, and any coefficient interpretation cells. Flag which cells must be REMOVED vs KEPT per Task 3A instructions. | `source_notebook: Current notebooks/Logistics.ipynb` | `lr_map` | — |
| **1c** | `notebook_mapper` | Map `GAM_notebook.ipynb` — identify: data load, internal ColumnTransformer, internal StratifiedKFold, PyGAMClassifier wrapper (cells 11–12), hyperparameter grid (cell 14), CV loop (cell 16), threshold tuning (cells 21–22), partial dependence plots (cell 30), and model save. Flag continuous_cols definition that needs fixing. | `source_notebook: Current notebooks/GAM_notebook.ipynb` | `gam_map` | — |
| **1d** | `notebook_mapper` | Map `unicef_fast_boosting_models_cv.ipynb` — identify: data load, internal OHE, MAX_ROWS_FOR_CV, CV_FOLDS, OOF threshold tuning block, make_one_hot_encoder(), and per-model (XGBoost/CatBoost/LightGBM) fitting sections. Flag XGBoost feature importance plot logic to KEEP. | `source_notebook: Current notebooks/unicef_fast_boosting_models_cv.ipynb` | `boost_map` | — |
| **1e** | `notebook_mapper` | Map `Final_nn_modeling_sklearn_v3.ipynb` — identify: cell 3 path block (REMOVE), internal pd.read_csv/train_test_split/preprocessor fitting (REMOVE), manual class-weight sweep (cells 12–14, REPLACE with RandomizedSearchCV), oversample_minority() helper (KEEP), probability distribution plot (cell 20, KEEP), threshold curve overlay (cell 21, KEEP). | `source_notebook: Current notebooks/Final_nn_modeling_sklearn_v3.ipynb` | `nn_map` | — |
| **1f** | `file-reading` | Read `RandomForest_v1.py` in full — extract the complete Python source. Identify: matplotlib.use("Agg") (REMOVE), `if __name__` block (REMOVE), internal data load + preprocessor (REMOVE), RandomizedSearchCV logic (KEEP), SHAP importance block (KEEP). | `source_file: Current notebooks/RandomForest_v1.py` | `rf_source` | — |
| **1g** | `file-reading` | Read `modelling_strategy_v2.txt` — extract: model grids per §4, expected F1 ranges, class imbalance levers, CV design, and threshold tuning protocol. | `source_file: Strategic_path/modelling_strategy_v2.txt` | `strategy_text` | — |
| **2** | `notebook_builder` ⚠️ | **Phase 0 + Phase 1**: Create `Unified_Notebook.ipynb`. Write cells for: (0) title markdown + global constants verbatim from instructions §0; (1.1) imports copied from `eda_map` cell 1 + path fix per instructions; (1.2) raw data load; (1.3) row cleaning — copy cells 9–16 per `eda_map`; (1.4) column cleaning — copy cells 19–65 per `eda_map`; (1.5) interaction engineering — copy cells 91–105; (1.6) save cleaned CSV + assertions; (1.7) 5 EDA visualisation cells.
